evidence: Augean strongly resonates with ontological.material (10) as it describes physical filth. Symmetry.destructive (10) reflects its opposition to cleanliness. Resonance.essential (7) captures the core essence of profound impurity. Scores in scale.ecosystem (5) and symmetry.contingent (4) reflect systemic neglect and dependency on conditions. Ethical value (4) arises from the moral dimension of neglect.

The word 'awful' strongly resonates with sensory perception (5) and material layer (5) due to its association with physical impurity. It also reflects ethical judgment (3) and aesthetic disapproval (4). The destructive symmetry (5) captures its negative valence, while essential resonance (5) ties it to core experiences of disgust. Scores remain low elsewhere as the term is tightly bound to direct sensory and moral reactions to dirt.

Bedraggled strongly resonates with physicality (ontological.material) and sensory perception (epistemological.perception). It implies a visible, surface-level dirtiness (resonance.superficial) tied to ethical neglect (value_generation.ethical). Its persistence (temporal.continuation) and dependency on conditions (lawfulness.contingent) also align with its common usage.

Befouled strongly resonates with physical material (ontological.material) and individual scale (scale.individual). Its sensory perception (epistemological.perception) and direct experience (epistemological.experience) are central. The destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive) reflects its negative valence. Aesthetic and ethical scores reflect impurity's moral and beauty implications. Superficial resonance (resonance.superficial) highlights its surface-level uncleanliness.

Begrimed directly resonates with its atom's material, individual, and independent nature. It persists over time (continuation), is perceived/identified, and embodies the destructive opposite of clean. Its essential resonance and predictable persistence align with the PRP.dirty definition.

The word 'black' strongly resonates with sensory perception (e.g., visual dirt) and material existence (physical uncleanliness). It aligns with destructive symmetry (impurity as a flaw) and superficial resonance (surface-level dirt). Moderate scores reflect its role as an indicator of dirt (temporal.indication), its persistence over time (temporal.continuation), and ethical implications (PRP.dirty's moral dimension).

The highest scores reflect the physical presence of bugs (material 10), their destructive impact on cleanliness (destructive 5), visible dirt (superficial 5), practical need for cleaning (functional 3), and common application to individual items (individual 5).

Cobwebby strongly resonates with physical material (dust/spiderwebs) and sensory perception (visually obvious dirt). Its persistence over time (continuation) and negative connotation (destructive symmetry) align with PRP.dirty. Scores drop to 0 for non-physical or abstract slots.

Dingy strongly resonates with materiality (physical dirt), perception (visually unclean), and destructive symmetry (as a negative state). It moderately connects to individual/community scale (common in small spaces), aesthetic value (lack of beauty), and ethical implications (neglect). Scores above 3 are grounded in direct associations with physical uncleanliness and its observable effects.

Dirt strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material), perception (epistemological.perception), and impurity's ethical/aesthetic implications (value_generation.ethical/aesthetic). Its destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive) and superficial resonance (resonance.superficial) reflect its role as an unclean substance opposed to cleanliness.

Dirtiness strongly resonates with its physical presence (material 10), its identification as impurity (identification 9), and its negative impact (destructive 9). It affects aesthetics (aesthetic 7) and practical needs (functional 6). Its essential resonance (8) ties to core impurity, while superficial aspects (7) reflect surface-level uncleanliness.

The word 'dirty' strongly resonates with ontological.material (10) due to its focus on physical uncleanliness. It scores highly in scale.individual (8) as dirt typically applies to objects or surfaces. Value.aesthetic (7) reflects dirt's negative impact on beauty. Epistemological.perception (7) and experience (4) highlight sensory awareness of dirt. Symmetry.destructive (5) aligns with dirt's association with harm, while catalytic (3) reflects its potential to trigger negative consequences (e.g., disease).

The word 'dirty-faced' strongly resonates with scale.individual (face is a personal attribute), ontological.material (physical dirt), interconnection.independent (state exists without external dependency), resonance.superficial (surface-level impurity), symmetry.destructive (negative counterpart to cleanliness), lawfulness.predictable (a known, observable state), and epistemological.perception (directly visible).

Draggled strongly resonates with physical materiality (ontological.material), the negative/dirty opposite (symmetry.destructive), and direct sensory perception (epistemological.perception). It also reflects persistence over time (temporal.continuation/influence) and a superficial layer of uncleanliness (resonance.superficial).

Feculent strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material) and the core essence of impurity (resonance.essential). It directly connects to sensory perception (epistemological.perception) and individual-scale dirtiness (scale.individual). Its destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive) and ties to ethical concerns (value_generation.ethical) further highlight its resonance.

Filth strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material) and its essential impurity (resonance.essential). It operates at individual/community/ecosystem scales and spreads catalytically (interconnection.catalytic). Its destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive) and ethical implications (value_generation.ethical) are central. Persistence (temporal.continuation) and sensory perception (epistemological.perception) are key.

Filthiness strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material), sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and negative impact (symmetry.destructive). It operates at individual/community/society/ecosystem scales and carries ethical implications (value_generation.ethical). Its superficial nature (resonance.superficial) contrasts with its essential core (resonance.essential).

Filthy strongly resonates with material impurity (ontological.material=10), sensory perception (epistemological.perception=7), and opposition to cleanliness (symmetry.destructive=7). It operates at individual/community scales (scale.individual=8, scale.community=5) and connects to aesthetic judgments (value_generation.aesthetic=5). Scores reflect its physical, relational, and contingent nature, avoiding overreach into abstract or existential domains.

Flyblown strongly resonates with destructive symmetry (7) as it denotes decay and pathology. Its material ontology (7) reflects physical dirt/flies. Temporal transformation (5) captures fundamental change via decomposition. Scale.individual (5) ties to isolated objects (e.g., a carcass). Epistemological identification (5) reflects the term's precise semantic meaning for this specific impurity.

Foul resonates strongly with sensory perception (smell/touch), identification as impurity, visceral experience, material/dirty substance, and the core essence of impurity. It aligns with destructive symmetry (negative quality) and ethical judgment (moral wrongness). Scores drop where connections are indirect or require overreach.

Fouled strongly resonates with material (physical dirt), destructive (negative state), perception (direct sensory awareness), and ethical (moral implications of impurity). Moderate scores reflect catalytic effects (e.g., fouling causing further damage) and superficial resonance (surface-level uncleanliness).

Foulness strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material=10) and its sensory perception (epistemological.perception=7). It carries ethical judgment (value_generation.ethical=6) and destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive=8), reflecting its core essence (resonance.essential=7). Scores are low for cosmic scales and existential resonance, as it is grounded in physical impurity rather than abstract systems or universal principles.

The word 'greasy' strongly resonates with physical material (ontological.material) as it describes a tangible, unclean substance. It is clearly identifiable (epistemological.identification) and directly perceptible (epistemological.perception). Its negative, destructive connotation (symmetry.destructive) aligns with PRP.dirty's impurity. Aesthetic and ethical scores reflect its negative sensory and moral associations.

Grimy strongly resonates with sensory perception (10), materiality (10), and superficiality (8), as it directly describes a tangible, surface-level unclean state. It contrasts with PRP.clean's symmetry, emphasizing impurity's destructive (6) and ethical implications (2).

Grubby strongly resonates with physical material (ontological.material), direct sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and its superficial, unthreatening nature (resonance.superficial). It reflects destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive) as a negative state and aligns with predictable patterns of dirt accumulation (lawfulness.predictable). Weak ties include individual scale (scale.individual) for personal contexts, ethical undertones (value_generation.ethical), and independent existence (interconnection.independent).

Grungy strongly resonates with materiality (ontological.material), superficial perception (resonance.superficial), and the destructive symmetry of uncleanliness. It persists over time (temporal.continuation) and is tied to ethical judgments (value_generation.ethical). Scores are low elsewhere due to its localized, sensory focus and lack of systemic or existential implications.

The strongest scores reflect the physical (material) nature of dirt obscuring legibility, its superficial surface-level manifestation, and its destructive impact on meaning. Catalytic resonance arises when illegibility prompts action to restore cleanliness.

Impure strongly resonates with symmetry.destructive (10) as it opposes PRP.clean, ontological.material (10) due to its physical nature, and resonance.superficial (8) as it often denotes surface-level uncleanliness. Epistemological.perception (6) and identification (5) reflect direct sensory detection and categorization. Value_generation.ethical (4) arises in contexts where impurity carries moral implications.

Impureness strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material), sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and the destructive aspect of impurity (symmetry.destructive). It connects to scale.individual/community/society/ecosystem as dirt affects objects, groups, and environments. Its superficial nature (resonance.superficial) and ethical implications (value_generation.ethical) are also prominent.

The word 'infected' strongly resonates with destructive opposition to cleanliness (symmetry.destructive=10), physical impurity (ontological.material=10), and individual-scale manifestation (scale.individual=10). It also reflects ethical implications (value.ethical=5) and catalytic spread (interconnection.catalytic=7). Temporal aspects like ongoing influence (temporal.influence=7) and continuation (temporal.continuation=8) highlight its persistent nature.

Lousiness strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material 7) and its superficial, visible nature (resonance.superficial 5). It is inherently destructive (symmetry.destructive 6) and tied to predictable neglect (lawfulness.predictable 3). Perception and embodied experience (epistemological.perception 5, epistemological.experience 3) ground its presence, while its ethical implications (value_generation.ethical 2) and persistence over time (temporal.continuation 2) further define its resonance.

The word 'lousy' strongly resonates with material impurity (ontological.material), sensory perception of dirt (epistemological.perception), and ethical judgment (value_generation.ethical). Its destructive symmetry opposes cleanliness, while superficial resonance reflects surface-level uncleanliness. Catalytic interconnection arises if dirt triggers further issues, and predictable lawfulness applies in expected contexts.

Maculate strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material), sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and its superficial nature (resonance.superficial). It opposes PRP.clean but directly aligns with physical impurity. Scores for ethical value reflect potential moral implications of neglect or uncleanliness.

The word 'marked-up' strongly resonates with the physical layer (ontological.material:10) as it describes tangible unclean substances. It aligns with superficial resonance (resonance.superficial:7) since dirt is surface-level. The destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive:6) reflects impurity's negative aspect. Predictable lawfulness (lawfulness.predictable:5) ties to expected conditions for dirt. Ethical value (value_generation.ethical:4) connects to moral implications of cleanliness.

Mucky strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material 10) and direct sensory perception (epistemological.perception 10). It has aesthetic negative value (value.aesthetic 7) and ethical implications (value.ethical 3). The state is independent (interconnection.independent 7) and superficial (resonance.superficial 8), though it touches the essential nature of impurity (resonance.essential 5). Predictable neglect leads to permanence (lawfulness.predictable 5), and its presence depends on conditions (lawfulness.contingent 3).

Muddy strongly resonates with material existence (10) as it describes a physical state. Its essential resonance (7) ties to core dirtiness, while superficial resonance (6) reflects surface-level uncleanliness. Predictable (6) because mud forms under expected conditions (e.g., wet soil). Individual (5) because it often describes single objects (e.g., muddy shoes).

Nastiness strongly resonates with material existence (dirt as physical uncleanliness), destructive symmetry (as a negative trait), and ethical implications (moral judgment of impurity). It also ties to sensory perception (seeing/feeling dirt) and lived experience (embodied awareness of filth). The semantic layer reflects its core meaning as impurity, while superficial resonance captures surface-level uncleanliness.

Nasty strongly resonates with ontological.material (physical impurity), symmetry.destructive (opposite of clean), and epistemological.perception (sensory awareness of dirt). Moderate scores reflect its influence over time and individual-scale applications. Lower scores align with its focus on tangible uncleanliness rather than abstract concepts.

Nonkosher strongly resonates with ethical value (5) and material ontology (5), reflecting its moral and physical impurity. It connects to destructive symmetry (3) as a violation and superficial resonance (3) through visible dirt. Scores reflect direct sensory perception (4), categorization (5), and semantic meaning (4). Lower scores reflect its limited scale (individual/society) and lack of existential or structural resonance.

Oily strongly resonates with ontological.material (oil is physical) and epistemological.perception (visually/tactilely evident). It weakly connects to destructive symmetry (as a negative trait) and scale.individual (common on objects/people). Scores above 0 reflect direct ties to physicality, sensory detection, and minor relational effects like contamination spread.

Pestiferous strongly resonates with physical material (ontological.material) and its perception (epistemological.perception). Its destructive nature (symmetry.destructive) and ethical implications (value_generation.ethical) align with impurity. Superficial resonance (resonance.superficial) reflects surface-level dirt, while contingent lawfulness (lawfulness.contingent) ties to dependency on pests.

Ratty strongly resonates with destructive symmetry (its negative, unclean essence opposes PRP.clean), material layer (physical dirt), and sensory perception (direct observation/touch of grime). It also connects to ethical implications (neglect or carelessness) and predictable processes (deterioration over time).

Raunchy strongly resonates with physical impurity (ontological.material) and its negative, destructive connotations (symmetry.destructive). It also aligns with surface-level dirt (resonance.superficial) and ethical implications of impurity (value.ethical).

Scummy strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material=5) and superficial presence (resonance.superficial=4). It contrasts with cleanliness (symmetry.destructive=4) and implies neglect (value.ethical=3). Persistence over time (temporal.continuation=2) and dependency on conditions (lawfulness.contingent=2) are also notable.

Septic strongly resonates with ontological.material (physical contamination), symmetry.destructive (pathological state), and resonance.essential (core impurity). It also connects to temporal transformation (fundamental change due to infection) and value.functional (medical necessity).

The word 'smudgy' strongly resonates with material physicality (ontological.material), sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and superficial surface-level dirt (resonance.superficial). It also ties to individual objects (scale.individual), persistence without intervention (lawfulness.predictable), and the functional need for cleaning (value_generation.functional).

Smutty strongly resonates with material impurity (ontological.material 8), sensory perception (epistemological.perception 7), and moral judgment (value_generation.ethical 6). It weakly connects to individual scale (5), experiential awareness (5), and destructive symmetry (7). Lower scores reflect its focus on physical impurity rather than systemic or existential aspects.

The word 'snot-nosed' strongly resonates with physical matter (ontological.material), individual people (scale.individual), and surface-level impurity (resonance.superficial). It is predictably linked to dirt (lawfulness.predictable) and perceived visually (epistemological.perception). Its negative connotation aligns with destructive relational dynamics (symmetry.destructive).

Snotty resonates most strongly with physical materiality (ontological.material), sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and ethical implications (value_generation.ethical). Its superficial dirtiness (resonance.superficial) and destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive) align with the PRP.dirty atom's focus on unclean impurity.

The word 'soiled' strongly resonates with ontological.material (10) as it directly describes physical uncleanliness. It aligns with epistemological.perception (8) and identification (7) due to its sensory and categorical nature. Symmetry.destructive (6) reflects its association with negative, impure states. Resonance.superficial (7) captures the surface-level nature of dirt, while ethical value (5) ties to moral implications of impurity.

Sooty strongly resonates with material impurity (ontological.material 10) and superficial dirt (resonance.superficial 5). It connects to destructive effects (symmetry.destructive 5) and predictable origins (lawfulness.predictable 5). Scores are low elsewhere due to its concrete, physical nature and lack of abstract or systemic implications.

Sordid strongly resonates with material impurity (ontological.material=10) and its ethical condemnation (value_generation.ethical=8). It reflects core essence (resonance.essential=8) and destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive=10). Scores in temporal categories (transformation, continuation) and scale (individual, society) reflect persistence and localized impact. Perception (epistemological.perception=7) and identification (epistemological.identification=8) highlight its direct sensory and categorical nature.

Squalid strongly resonates with material reality (ontological.material) and sensory perception (epistemological.perception). Its ethical judgment (value_generation.ethical) and destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive) reflect its negative valuation. Persistence (temporal.continuation) and transformation (temporal.transformation) highlight its temporal endurance and causal chains (interconnection.chained).

Terefah strongly resonates with material impurity (ontological.material) and sensory perception (epistemological.perception). It carries destructive connotations (symmetry.destructive) and operates on an individual scale (scale.individual). Superficial resonance (resonance.superficial) and predictability (lawfulness.predictable) reflect its tangible, observable nature. Ethical implications (value_generation.ethical) arise from its association with moral impurity.

The word 'travel-soiled' strongly resonates with physical materiality (ontological.material), sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and individual scale (scale.individual). Its superficial nature (resonance.superficial) and predictable association with travel (lawfulness.predictable) are central. Scores drop where connections are abstract or require stretching the atom's definition.

The word 'travel-stained' strongly resonates with physical dirt (ontological.material 10) and individual scale (8). It is visually perceived (9) and identified (7) as a state of impurity from travel. The stains are essential (7) to the concept and contingent (5) on travel. Functional (3) and aesthetic (5) value arise from practical wear or charming appearance. The dirt may carry a mild destructive connotation (2) as impurity.

The strongest resonances are with ontological.material (physical dirt), resonance.essential (core essence of impurity), symmetry.destructive (negative connotation), epistemological.perception (direct sensory awareness of dirt), and value_generation.ethical (moral dimension of cleanliness). Scores reflect the tangible, sensory, and moral aspects of being dirty, while avoiding over-scoring abstract or systemic connections.

Unclean strongly resonates with direct sensory perception (sight/smell) and material layer (physical dirt). It has moral/ethical implications (e.g., impurity in religious contexts) and catalyzes negative outcomes (e.g., health risks). Its presence depends on conditions (contingent) and reduces functional/aesthetic value. Scores reflect its tangible, experiential, and relational aspects without overextending to abstract or systemic scales.

Uncleanly strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material 9), its destructive opposition to cleanliness (symmetry.destructive 7), and ethical implications of impurity (value_generation.ethical 6). It also reflects surface-level perception (resonance.superficial 4) and is tied to individual scale (scale.individual 6). Lower scores reflect its lack of systemic or existential depth beyond immediate physical and moral contexts.

Uncleanness strongly resonates with material existence (physical dirt), sensory perception (direct awareness of impurity), destructive opposition to cleanliness, superficial surface-level impurity, and ethical/moral value judgments about purity. Scores peak in these areas while other slots remain neutral.

Unswept strongly resonates with being a physical state (ontological.material), its opposition to cleanliness (symmetry.destructive), and its observable nature (epistemological.perception). It also reflects a predictable persistence (lawfulness.predictable) and practical implications (value.functional).

The word 'untouchable' in the context of PRP.dirty strongly resonates with materiality (physical impurity), ethical judgment (moral impurity), and destructive symmetry (negative state). It also connects to temporal persistence (continuation, permanence) and epistemological perception (direct sensory awareness of dirt).

Unwashed strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material), sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and individual scale (scale.individual). Its negative connotation aligns with destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive). Ethical implications arise from societal norms around cleanliness, while aesthetic value reflects judgments about impurity.
